hypothesis: In RANGE regime (low ADX, mean-reverting price action), BTCUSDT prices revert to short-term central tendency. Entering contrarian positions at z-score extremes (oversold/overbought) and exiting at mean reversion can produce positive net expectancy given controlled friction costs. This hypothesis is fundamentally distinct from primary_breakout_v1 which requires TREND regime and trend-following entry logic.
risk_assumptions: Core Risk Service, kill-switch, and LR gates remain authoritative.
Candidate evidence cannot override runtime risk blocks.
Mean reversion in low-volume regime transitions may produce extended losses if regime shifts to TREND mid-trade.
long_short direction requires valid short signal execution path in replay.
execution_assumptions: Fees, spread, and slippage must be accounted for before ranking.
Existing MEXC same-venue data is available for file-backed replay.
Backtest, ARVP, and paper evidence do not imply live execution readiness.
Short-side execution in replay simulator must produce valid fills.

unsafe_zones.reason: LR remains NO-GO and this contract is research-only.
Mean reversion positions held during TREND or HIGH_VOL_CHAOTIC regime transitions may produce extended adverse moves.
Short-side execution realism is unvalidated in the current replay infrastructure.

limitations: No productive DB registry is created by this draft.
Parameter set is initial research estimate; requires calibration from replay evidence.
RANGE regime detection uses existing ADX-based regime service thresholds.
Controlled_lab_evidence only until natural-paper replay path exists.
No DB, runtime capture, or code change occurred in this selection slice.
